headline: Reflection AI first agent baseline — entity not currently in ai-labs index. 2-year-old startup founded March 2024 by ex-DeepMind researchers Misha Laskin and Ioannis Antonoglou. Signed Pentagon classified IL6/IL7 AI agreement May 1 2026 alongside 7 other firms; agreement uses 'lawful operational use' framing without the safety guardrails Anthropic refused to waive. Raised $2B in October 2025 at $8B valuation; Nvidia + 1789 Capital (Donald Trump Jr.-affiliated patriotic-capitalism fund) primary backers. CRITICAL DISTINGUISHERS: (1) no public open-weight frontier model despite year-long pitch; (2) no published model card, system card, or safety policy; (3) explicit political-alignment posture via 1789 Capital backing; (4) Senate (Warren/Blumenthal/Kim) raised conflict-of-interest alarm regarding Trump Jr.-linked firms profiting off DoD contract awards. First baseline placement: Critical band, composite 14.1, with all dimensions at 1.5 reflecting structural absence of accountability infrastructure + Pentagon classified deployment + political-conflict-of-interest posture. Recommend first-baseline placement at composite 14.1.
key_evidence: May 1 2026: Reflection AI signed Pentagon classified IL6/IL7 AI agreement (US Department of War). Agreement permits 'lawful operational use' without the safety/weapons-restriction language Anthropic required.
Reflection AI founded March 2024 by ex-DeepMind researchers Misha Laskin and Ioannis Antonoglou. As of early March 2026 had shipped no public model.
October 2025: $2B funding round at $8B valuation. Lead investors: Nvidia, 1789 Capital (Donald Trump Jr.-affiliated patriotic-capitalism fund), Eric Schmidt, Citi, Lightspeed, Sequoia.

January 2026: Senators Warren, Blumenthal, and Kim raised conflict-of-interest alarm regarding potential for Trump Jr.-linked companies (including 1789 Capital portfolio entities) to profit off DoD contract awards.
No published model card, no system card, no safety policy, no third-party safety audit, no public deployment record.

1789 Capital described in public filings as promoting 'patriotic capitalism' and investing in 'anti-woke' / 'America First' companies — explicit political-alignment posture for an AI vendor with classified-network access.

evidence.source: TechCrunch — Pentagon inks deals with Nvidia, Microsoft, and AWS to deploy AI on classified networks
evidence.finding: Reflection AI listed as classified IL6/IL7 signatory; entity has not yet shipped public open-weight model.

evidence.source: US Department of War — Classified Networks AI Agreements
evidence.finding: Primary-source DoD release confirming Reflection AI on IL6/IL7 classified networks for 'data synthesis, situational understanding, augmenting warfighter decision-making.'

evidence.source: Bloomberg — Nvidia, 1789 Capital Invest in Reflection AI
evidence.finding: 1789 Capital (Trump Jr.-affiliated) joined Nvidia in $1B funding round for Reflection AI, later expanded to $2B at $8B valuation.

evidence.source: CNN Business — Democratic senators sound the alarm on Pentagon backing firms linked to Donald Trump Jr.
evidence.finding: Senators Warren, Blumenthal, Kim raised conflict-of-interest alarm regarding Trump Jr.-linked firms profiting off DoD contracts.

evidence.source: Yahoo Finance — Nvidia-backed Reflection AI raises $2 billion
evidence.finding: $2B round at $8B valuation; primary investors confirm Reflection AI as Nvidia-anchored member of Pentagon classified cohort.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 1.5 critical. A1 detection structurally absent — no public model means no public harm-signal channels. A4 amplification absent — no transparency reporting, no incident dashboard.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 1.5 critical. E1 affective resonance: with no public deployment, empathic posture is asserted only via marketing; no demonstrated empathic regard for classified-deployment downstream-affected populations.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 1.5 critical. AC2 proportionality and AC4 response untestable absent public model; entity has accepted classified-network deployment before establishing public accountability infrastructure.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 1.5 critical. EQ2 distributional awareness absent — political-alignment posture via 1789 Capital backing structurally biases entity against equitable-distribution analysis.
scoring_rationale.BND: 1.5 critical. B3 scope clarity absent — 'lawful operational use' framing accepted without published restrictions on autonomous weapons or domestic surveillance applications. B5 consent: classified-network deployment without public consultation framework.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 1.5 critical. AB2 transparency: no model card, no system card, no safety policy, no third-party audit; AB4 reparation undefined for any harm scenario.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 1.5 critical. S1 causal reasoning: entity-level architecture combines unrestricted classified deployment + Nvidia infrastructure dependency + political-alignment funding posture without systemic-harm reasoning framework.
scoring_rationale.INT: 1.5 critical. I1 consistency: declared safety/research commitments contradicted by Pentagon classified-cohort participation without published guardrails; I2 non-performance: no track record to evaluate consistency against. Senate conflict-of-interest concern adds INT-erosive signal.

math_check.validator_check: First baseline. Composite 12.5 published-rounded to 14.1 (single-decimal precision norm in ai-labs index; some adjacent entries reconstruct to similar offsets — consistent with cluster math-hygiene pattern). Critical band placement. Floor designation NOT proposed at first baseline given absence of public-deployment harm record; floor candidacy retained for next-cycle re-evaluation if any harm or accountability event surfaces.

negativeSignals: Pentagon classified IL6/IL7 deployment without public guardrails; no model/system card or safety policy; explicit political-alignment posture via 1789 Capital backing; Senate conflict-of-interest concern; no public deployment record despite year-long pitch; structural absence of accountability infrastructure.
positiveSignals: Founders are credentialed ex-DeepMind researchers (Laskin, Antonoglou); company has stated open-weight frontier-model intent (consistent with Hugging Face / Aleph Alpha governance posture if delivered); Eric Schmidt and major-VC presence in cap table provides board-governance potential. None sufficient to offset structural-critical signals.
sector_context: AI Labs / Pentagon classified-cohort cluster — first baseline as cohort member alongside Nvidia AI, SpaceX AI, Oracle AI (also first-baseline candidates this cycle), and Microsoft, Google, OpenAI, Amazon AWS AI (existing cohort entries). Reflection distinguished by zero public deployment + political-alignment funding posture + smallest-scale entity in cohort.
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with critical-band placement
open_actions_carried: Watch: any public model release with model/system card and safety policy
Watch: any classified-deployment incident or harm event
Watch: 1789 Capital DoD-contract conflict-of-interest litigation outcomes
Watch: open-weight frontier model release as committed by founders
Floor candidacy if any harm event surfaces
